translation.text: The 3:24pm train from Huntington due Penn Station at 4:31pm departed 13 minutes late due to a late-arriving connection.
The 3:24pm train from Huntington due Penn Station at 4:31pmdeparted 13 minutes late due to a late-arriving connection.
Trains run every 2 hours, bypass Hicksville and Bethpage, and take up to an hour longer than normal
Trains run every 2 hours, bypass Hicksville and Bethpage, and take up to an hour longer than normal

Bi-level diesel trains will run every two hours between Penn Station and Ronkonkoma while we test signals near Carle Place as part of the LIRR Expansion Project. Between Jamaica and Farmingdale, they will travel via Babylon and will change directions twice, but not make any station stops. This means that your trip will take up to an hour longer than normal.
 
If you typically use Bethpage, or travel between Hicksville and points east of Farmingdale, a bus will run between Mineola, Hicksville, Bethpage, and Farmingdale.
 
If possible, consider using the Babylon or Montauk Branches. Detailed schedule information will be available as the work approaches.

If possible, consider using the Babylon or Montauk Branches. Detailed schedule information will be available as the work approaches.
Jan 29 - 31 and Feb 5 - 7, Sat 12:00 AM to Mon 4:00 AM
Buses replace trains between Mineola and Huntington
Buses replace trains between Mineola and Huntington

Trains operate in two sections while we test signals near Carle Place as part of the LIRR Expansion Project:
Between Penn Station and Mineola every hour
Between Huntington and Port Jefferson every two hours
Buses will run between Mineola and Huntington, stopping at Hicksville, Syosset, Cold Spring Harbor, and Huntington. If possible, consider using the Hempstead, Babylon, or Montauk Branches, as buses will take up to an hour longer than normal train schedules. NICE Bus is also accepting LIRR tickets on the n22 and n24 bus routes between Hicksville and Mineola, with stops near Carle Place and Westbury stations.

Detailed schedule information will be available as the work approaches.

Trains operate in two sections while we test signals near Carle Place as part of the LIRR Expansion Project:

  • Between Penn Station and Mineola every hour

  • Between Huntington and Port Jefferson every two hours

Buses will run between Mineola and Huntington, stopping at Hicksville, Syosset, Cold Spring Harbor, and Huntington. If possible, consider using the Hempstead, Babylon, or Montauk Branches, as buses will take up to an hour longer than normal train schedules. NICE Bus is also accepting LIRR tickets on the n22 and n24 bus routes between Hicksville and Mineola, with stops near Carle Place and Westbury stations.

Detailed schedule information will be available as the work approaches.
Jan 29 - 31 and Feb 5 - 7, Sat 12:00 AM to Mon 4:00 AM
Adjusted connections for two overnight trains
Adjusted connections for two overnight trains

If you currently take the 1:47 AM from Far Rockaway, your connecting train to Woodside and Penn Station will leave at 2:32 instead of 2:24. If you are going to Kew Gardens or Forest Hills, your connection will leave at 2:41.
If you currently take the 3:15 AM from Penn and change at Jamaica for Far Rockaway, you will need to leave 5 minutes earlier on the 3:10 Babylon train. From Woodside, you will need to take the Long Beach train at 3:11.
The LIRR Expansion Project is testing signals near Carle Place.

• If you currently take the 1:47 AM from Far Rockaway, your connecting train to Woodside and Penn Station will leave at 2:32 instead of 2:24. If you are going to Kew Gardens or Forest Hills, your connection will leave at 2:41.

The LIRR Expansion Project is testing signals near Carle Place.

Jan 10 - 14, Jan 17 - 21, and Jan 24 - 28, Mon to Fri
Service increased to half-hourly
Service increased to half-hourly

We are operating Hempstead Branch service every half hour to provide an alternative to the Port Jefferson Branch while we install switches near Queens Village. Every other train operates to/from Penn Station.

To plan your trip, use the LIRR TrainTime app or the special PDF timetable.

To plan your trip, use the LIRR TrainTime app or the special PDF timetable.
Jan 22 - 23, Sat and Sun, 6:30 AM to 11:59 PM
Minor schedule and connection adjustments
Minor schedule and connection adjustments

Special timetables are in effect systemwide while we install switches near Queens Village. While there are no major changes to service on this branch, there are minor changes in departure and connecting train times that may affect your trip.

To plan your trip, use the LIRR TrainTime app or the special PDF timetable.

Weekend of January 22-23
No trains between Jamaica and East Williston
No trains between Jamaica and East Williston

Trains run every two hours between Oyster Bay and East Williston while we install switches near Queens Village. Shuttle buses connect East Williston, Mineola, and Hempstead, where train service is available to Jamaica, Penn Station, and Atlantic Terminal. Click here to see a map of this service change.

Please allow up to 60 minutes of additional travel time or consider using the Port Washington or Hempstead Branches instead.

The last through trains to operate on Friday night are the 11:26 PM from Oyster Bay and the 12:30 AM from Jamaica. Service resumes with the 1:42 AM from Jamaica on Monday morning.

Detailed schedule information will be available as the work approaches.

To plan your trip, use the LIRR TrainTime app or the special PDF timetable.

Jan 22 - 24, Sat 1:00 AM to Mon 12:30 AM
No trains between Jamaica and Hicksville
No trains between Jamaica and Hicksville

Trains run in four sections while we install switches near Queens Village:
Between Greenport and Ronkonkoma
Between Ronkonkoma and Hicksville (bypassing Bethpage)
Between Hicksville and Babylon (stopping at Bethpage)
Between Babylon and Jamaica/Penn Station
Transfer at Hicksville and Babylon for continuing service. Please allow up to 60 minutes of additional travel time or consider using the Babylon or Montauk Branches instead. Click here to see a map of this service change.

Bethpage riders: To travel towards Jamaica, wait on Platform B for a bi-level train to Babylon. To travel towards Ronkonkoma, wait on Platform A for a bi-level train to Hicksville. Use caution when crossing the tracks at Stewart Ave.

The last through trains to operate on Friday night are the 11:14 PM from Penn Station and the 11:16 PM from Ronkonkoma. Service resumes with the 12:14 AM from Penn Station and 12:45 AM from Ronkonkoma on Monday morning.

To plan your trip, use the LIRR TrainTime app or the special PDF timetable.

Jan 22 - 24, Sat 12:15 AM to Mon 12:30 AM
No trains between Jamaica and Hicksville
No trains between Jamaica and Hicksville

Hicksville through Port Jefferson
Trains run in three sections while we install switches near Queens Village:
Between Huntington or Port Jefferson and Hicksville
Between Hicksville and Babylon
Between Babylon and Jamaica/Penn Station
Transfer at Hicksville and Babylon for continuing service. Please allow up to 60 minutes of additional travel time or consider using the Babylon or Montauk Branches instead. Click here to see a map of this service change.
New Hyde Park through Westbury
There is no service at these stations. Consider using the Babylon or Hempstead Branches instead, which will have increased service all weekend. NICE Bus is accepting LIRR tickets on the n22 and n24 bus routes between Hicksville and Mineola, and on the n40/41 bus route between Mineola and Hempstead. See nicebus.com for schedules. Between midnight and 5 AM, when NICE Bus does not run, special shuttle buses will be provided between Hicksville and Mineola. See an LIRR employee to board the bus.

The last through trains to operate on Friday night are the 10:52 PM from Port Jefferson, the 11:22 PM from Huntington, and the 11:39 PM from Penn Station. Service resumes with the 12:19 AM from Huntington and 1:11 AM from Penn Station on Monday morning.

To plan your trip, use the LIRR TrainTime app or the special PDF timetable.

Reminder: off peak fares are accepted on all trains through the end of February.

Monday, January 17
Parking restrictions at New Hyde Park
Parking restrictions at New Hyde Park

There is no parking along 3rd Avenue between Covert Avenue and South 12th Street. These closures facilitate the construction of a new pedestrian underpass and sound walls.

We expect that the parking spots will reopen by winter 2022. As an alternative, use the new metered parking lot on the north side of the tracks between New Hyde Park Road and Herkomer Street.

Click here to see a map of the station.

There is no parking along 3rd Avenue between Covert Avenue and South 12th Street. These closures facilitate the construction of a new pedestrian underpass and sound walls.

We expect that the parking spots will reopen by winter 2022. As an alternative, use the new metered parking lot on the north side of the tracks between New Hyde Park Road and Herkomer Street.

Click here to see a map of the station.
Until Winter 2022
You must be in the last eight cars to exit at Huntington
You must be in the last eight cars to exit at Huntington

The east end of both station platforms is closed while the Town of Huntington replaces the eastern pedestrian overpass. When complete, the new enclosed overpass will feature glass panel walls, brighter LED lighting, and CCTV security cameras.
 
In addition, the south parking lot is closed for renovation work by the Town of Huntington. The parking lot project will include 29 new parking spaces, a new accessible sidewalk on the east side of Fairground Avenue, drainage improvements, lighting improvements, resurfacing with new asphalt paving, and reconstruction of the southern retaining wall. We expect the lot to reopen during the 4th quarter of 2021. To accommodate this work, two staircases leading from the lot to the platform will also temporarily close. The remaining staircases, accessibility ramp, and access to the parking garage will remain open. During construction, vehicles must access the south parking garage by the southern entrance from Second Street, and the eastern entrance from Lenox Road. There will be no access to the garage from Fairground Avenue.
 
Click here to see a map of the station.

Until late 2022
Elevator to Tracks 20/21 at Penn Station closed
Elevator to Tracks 20/21 at Penn Station closed

We are replacing the elevator as part of the Penn Station LIRR Concourse project. Customers with disabilities may use a chair lift that has been installed on the staircase closest to the ticket office. When you arrive at the lift, please push the attendant call button for assistance.
 
Click here to see a map of the concourse.

We are replacing the elevator as part of the Penn Station LIRR Concourse project. Customers with disabilities may use a chair lift that has been installed on the staircase closest to the ticket office. When you arrive at the lift, please push the attendant call button for assistance.

Click here to see a map of the concourse.
Until early 2022
You must be in the first six cars to exit at Westbury
You must be in the first six cars to exit at Westbury

The existing platforms are closed while the station is renovated as part of the LIRR Expansion Project. Six-car temporary platforms are in place at the east end of the station. Ticket machines are located at the west end of the temporary platforms.

The pedestrian underpass is closed until the construction is complete. To cross the tracks, you must use the underpass at Post Avenue. Allow additional time to reach the platform and check the LIRR TrainTime App for track assignments before walking to the platforms. Track 3 is the north platform (generally westbound) and Track 1 is the south platform (generally eastbound).

Click here to see a map of the station.

Until further notice
You must be in the first 10 cars to exit westbound trains at Mineola
You must be in the first 10 cars to exit westbound trains at Mineola

The easternmost portion of Platform A (westbound/north side) is closed while the station is renovated as part of the LIRR Expansion Project. In addition, the station building/ticket office is closed and replaced with a temporary ticket office.

Parts of the station plaza and parking lots on both sides of the station are closed for construction. To cross the tracks, you must use the Mineola Intermodal Center overpass located west of the open platforms. Please allow additional time to reach the platform.

Note: If you are riding the 5:33 PM weekday train from Penn Station to Hicksville, you will also need to be in the first 10 cars to exit at Mineola as this train arrives on the north side platform.

Click here to see a map of the station.

Event service only

Every day

Until further notice
Westbound Brooklyn trains depart Jamaica 15 minutes later
Westbound Brooklyn trains depart Jamaica 15 minutes later

Westbound trains will wait in Jamaica for 15 minutes before continuing to Brooklyn while we conduct routine track inspections. If you are traveling from the Far Rockaway or Hempstead Branches, please remain on the train at Jamaica as there is no need to transfer. Eastbound service is unaffected.

Tuesdays, 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM, through March 2022
